Guys one question, as a new player after tawil e2 should I be getting doll:inferno or wait for new banners?
What’s the math behind the expected number of pulls being 42?
It's a statistical probability based on the normalized rate for a unit
OR in normal person words. You plug the values in a probability calculator and that’s what you get
I'm a researcher, statistics is like 80% of the job. 20% is talking to people who don't know what they're doing

Specifically, the rate at which you acquire units is calculated via a bell curve. Mathematically it just means that you are extremely likely (though not guaranteed) to get something in X amount of attempts based on the % chance you have to acquire that thing. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Normal_distribution

embeds.title: Normal distribution
embeds.description:
In probability theory and statistics, a normal distribution or Gaussian distribution is a type of continuous probability distribution for a real-valued random variable. The general form of its probability density function is

  
    
      
        f
        (
        x
        )
        =

content: How do you account for pity?
You simply introduce it as part of the distribution. Every X attempts you are guaranteed an SSR, that gets added into the distribution for likelihood
The rate for the thing you want is actually lower than that, as the 3% accounts for the entire pool, while the chance to pull the unit is lower than that at 2.38%
WoDs actually have a higher rate of 3.33%, meaning it's easier to max them out
The 3.03% is an adjusted rate, which means with the inclusion of the guarantee, it's the rate at which you will mathematically get it
By gacha standards, it's a very high rate

But you also tend to need multiple copies to get full functionality of units, so it's a tradeoff at the end of the day. Though, maxing units in this game is much cheaper than say, a Hoyo game or Chaos Zero Nightmare (which copies Hoyo's formula almost to the letter)
Most gacha games these days tend to copy Hoyo games (Genshin Impact / Zenless Zone Zero / Honkai Star Rail)
Finally was able to work it out at least in a simulation. I’ll try working out the math later
